summary: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on the Zaporizhzhia region and increased Shahed drone activity across multiple fronts. Ukraine reported a fatal drone interception incident in Khmelnytskyi and announced the liberation of several settlements in Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions. Alerts were issued for a MiG‑31K launch carrying an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile and for potential Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Vovchansk.
key_points: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
Shahed‑type drones were launched from Bryansk and Kursk regions (Source: @eRadarrua/94138).
Ukrainian forces liberated Budarki and Karaichnoye in Kharkiv and Novopidgorodne and Lisnoye in Dnipropetrovsk within 24 hours (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524).
Two Ukrainian servicemen killed and one wounded during a drone‑intercept mission in Khmelnytskyi (Source: @UkraineNow/81521).
Air alarm declared nationwide due to launch of MiG‑31K carrying X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile (Source: @eRadarrua/94142).
Threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from Vovchansk region reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Multiple Shahed drone sightings reported over Sumy, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Odesa directions (Source: @kpszsu/63189, @kpszsu/63206, @kpszsu/63202, @kpszsu/63191).

sections.executive_summary: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, intensifying air‑strike activity. Ukraine announced the liberation of four settlements in Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions, while suffering casualties in a drone‑intercept incident in Khmelnytskyi. Nationwide air alarms were issued following a MiG‑31K launch carrying a Kinzhal missile and warnings of Iskander‑M launches from Vovchansk.

points: Two Ukrainian servicemen were killed and one wounded while intercepting hostile drones during a combat mission in Khmelnytskyi; the incident is under investigation by the DBR (Source: @UkraineNow/81521).

The Ukrainian Ministry of Defense reported the liberation of Budarki and Karaichnoye in Kharkiv region and Novopidgorodne and Lisnoye in Dnipropetrovsk region within a 24‑hour period (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524).

Ukrainian forces conducted clearing operations near Novoselivka in Dnipropetrovsk, as well as around Voronno, Sichneve, Pidubne, Tovste, Novokhatke, and Zelenyi Hai, reportedly reducing enemy control by at least 46 km² (Source: @UkraineNow/81535).
Ukrainian special‑operations unit received the honorary title "Heroes of UPA" from the President, sparking criticism from Poland (Source: @UkraineNow/81532).
Ukraine plans to purchase up to 20 JAS‑39 Gripen E‑modification fighters and receive 16 older C/D variants from Sweden (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30505).
Zelensky warned the United States about a critical shortage in Ukraine's missile‑defence capabilities, emphasizing reliance on U.S. ballistic missile defence (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30502).
Japan announced deployment of four Self‑Defense Forces officers to the NATO‑Ukraine Security Assistance and Training Centre in Wiesbaden for training Ukrainian troops (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30526).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs over Zaporizhzhia region, indicating increased use of precision strike assets (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
Shahed‑type drones were reported launched from Russian Bryansk and Kursk regions, suggesting cross‑border UAV operations (Source: @eRadarrua/94138).
A MiG‑31K fighter, carrier of the X‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile, took off from Savalske airfield, prompting a nationwide air alarm (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67254).
Potential Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches were warned from Vovchansk area, raising concerns of long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Russian forces claimed to have liberated ten settlements in the SVO zone over the past week (Source: @tass_agency/378086).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the southern and north‑eastern directions, indicating broader air operations (Source: @kpszsu/63196, @kpszsu/63201).
A group of Shahed drones was observed over the Black Sea heading towards Izmail district and Orlivka/Reni, highlighting maritime UAV threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67256).
Ukrainian drones were sighted near Sumy heading towards Bilopillya, indicating reconnaissance or strike activity (Source: @kpszsu/63189).
Shahed drones were reported moving from the south of Kharkiv towards Slobozhanske, suggesting pressure on eastern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/63161).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near Chernihiv moving southward past Snovsk, indicating surveillance along the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/63202).
Multiple Shahed drone launches were reported from the Black Sea towards Odesa/Chornomorske, posing a maritime‑to‑land threat (Source: @kpszsu/63191).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity over the Izium direction from the east in Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63194).
Drone sightings over Dnipropetrovsk heading towards Petropavlivka from the east suggest pressure on central‑eastern axes (Source: @kpszsu/63192).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near the border of Kharkiv and Poltava heading towards Dnipropetrovsk via Zachepylivka (Source: @eRadarrua/94098).
UAVs were reported over Chernihiv heading south past Snovsk (Source: @kpszsu/63202).
UAV activity noted in northern Chernihiv moving towards Snovsk (Source: @kpszsu/63183).
Ukrainian UAVs observed from Belgorod region heading south into Sumy, indicating cross‑border incursions (Source: @kpszsu/63220).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑eastern direction, raising threat to front‑line positions (Source: @kpszsu/63201).
UAVs spotted over Chernihiv heading towards Gornost (likely Gorbov) suggesting reconnaissance (Source: @eRadarrua/94139).
Shahed drone alerts over the Black Sea towards Izmail district also affect northern coastal defenses (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67256).
UAV sightings over the northern front of Chernihiv near Snovsk indicate continued aerial pressure (Source: @kpszsu/63214).
Multiple Shahed drones reported over Odesa region and Black Sea heading towards Tartarabany and Izmail district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67256, @eRadarrua/94149).
UAVs observed over Odesa/Chornomorske from the Black Sea, indicating maritime‑to‑land threats (Source: @kpszsu/63191).
Russian UAV attacks on a multi‑storey building in Romania, a NATO member, were noted, suggesting spill‑over threats (Source: @eRadarrua/94105).
UAV activity reported over the southern direction of Kharkiv towards Izium, adding pressure to southern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/63194).
UAVs observed over the southern front in Sumy heading towards Konotop from the southeast (Source: @kpszsu/63219).
UAV sightings over the southern Odesa direction towards Orlivka/Reni indicate continued maritime threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
UAVs reported over the southern front of Dnipropetrovsk heading towards Petropavlivka, suggesting pressure on central‑southern axes (Source: @kpszsu/63192).
Nationwide air alarm declared due to MiG‑31K launch carrying X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile (Source: @eRadarrua/94142).
Potential Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch threat from Vovchansk region reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
Shahed‑type drones launched from Bryansk and Kursk indicate cross‑border UAV operations (Source: @eRadarrua/94138).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted a drone over Kyiv at 15:39, ending the air alarm (Source: @eRadarrua/94148).
Ukrainian forces reported successful interception of hostile drones in Khmelnytskyi, though with casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81521).
Ukrainian air force prepared to re‑equip with JAS‑39 Gripen fighters from Sweden, enhancing future air capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30505).

key_developments: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, indicating intensified precision strike operations (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
Shahed‑type drones were launched from Bryansk and Kursk regions, suggesting cross‑border UAV activity (Source: @eRadarrua/94138).
Ukraine reported the liberation of Budarki and Karaichnoye in Kharkiv and Novopidgorodne and Lisnoye in Dnipropetrovsk within 24 hours (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524).
Two Ukrainian servicemen were killed and one wounded during a drone‑intercept mission in Khmelnytskyi (Source: @UkraineNow/81521).
Nationwide air alarm was issued after a MiG‑31K launch carrying an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile (Source: @eRadarrua/94142).
Threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Vovchansk region was reported, raising long‑range strike concerns (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Multiple Shahed drones were observed over the Black Sea heading towards Izmail district and Orlivka/Reni, indicating maritime UAV threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67256).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air assets are increasingly employing precision guided munitions and hypersonic-capable platforms, as evidenced by guided bomb strikes in Zaporizhzhia and MiG‑31K/Kinzhal launches. Cross‑border Shahed drone operations from Russian territories and maritime UAV incursions into the Black Sea heighten the threat to Ukrainian rear areas and NATO member Romania. Ukrainian air‑defence interceptions remain active but face growing pressure from diversified aerial threats.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine continues offensive operations, reclaiming settlements in Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk while conducting extensive UAV reconnaissance across multiple fronts. International partners are augmenting capabilities, with Japan providing training personnel and Sweden offering Gripen fighters, while the United States remains the primary source of ballistic missile defence. NATO members, notably Romania, have experienced spill‑over drone attacks, prompting heightened alertness along alliance borders.
sections.intelligence_notes: The concentration of Shahed drone launches from Russian border regions suggests a coordinated effort to saturate Ukrainian air‑defence networks. The reported Iskander‑M launch threat from Vovchansk may indicate preparation for longer‑range strikes against strategic targets. Monitoring of maritime UAV trajectories from the Black Sea is critical, as they could be used to target coastal infrastructure and support amphibious operations.
